item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
item.description: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

item.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
item.description: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

item.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

item.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
item.description: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
item.description: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Long-awaited rapid bus plan fails to reveal construction timeline or routes
item.description: After about a year of delays, the Tasmanian government has released its strategic business case for the Greater Hobart Rapid Bus Network, but a lot of the detail has not been included.

item.title: Mackay SLSC Surf Boat Rowing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Winx's daughter, Quinceanera, at trackwork
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Jono Ridler's 90-day swim could inspire New Zealand to protect its oceans
item.description: Jono Ridler swam 1,367 kilometres down New Zealand's east coast to raise awareness for ocean health and to end bottom trawling. His exploits have galvanised a nation.

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: This island was once ruled by Australia. Now, it could become the world's newest country
item.description: A tropical archipelago just 1,500 kilometres from Australia is readying to become the world's newest country. But as Bougainville nears independence, memories of a bloody past are coming back to the surface.

item.title: Here's how federal budget negative gearing and capital gains tax changes will affect you
item.description: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

item.title: How a sister's love unraveled the tragic death of Olympic hopeful Ian Walker and unmasked the teacher who abused him
item.description: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

item.title: High-income earners use discretionary trusts to pay less tax, but will budget change have unintended consequences?
item.description: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

item.title: Matthew Wale voted in as new Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot
item.description: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

item.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
item.description: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.

item.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers in migration crackdown
item.description: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.

item.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen
item.description: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?

item.title: Wayne Swan slams the 'view from Mar-a-Lago' as battle for middle Australia gets real
item.description: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.

item.title: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy
item.description: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

item.title: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia
item.description: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

item.title: US President Donald Trump, Chinese President Xi Jinping end unipolar age in Beijing
item.description: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

item.title: Mature Artists Dance Experience for over 50s hopes for increased arts funding ahead of Tasmanian budget
item.description: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

item.title: The spiral-shaped trap: AI chatbots and the descent into delusion
item.description: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

item.title: Eurovision key moments: from Euro-pop to Euro-slop as Delta Goodrem makes Australia proud
item.description: Slept through Eurovision this year? Here's what you missed from Europe's favourite song contest, with boycotts, ballads and a Bulgarian song called Bangaranga making headlines.

item.title: Jim Chalmers defends impact of tax changes on young investors
item.description: Jim Chalmers says it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes, and argued they can still access negative gearing by buying up new homes.

item.title: The ripples of the Iran war are everywhere
item.description: It has been more than two months since the US and Israel launched strikes in Iran. But the impact of the conflict continues globally.

item.title: Australia's interest rate rise comes as other countries maintain theirs. Why?
item.description: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

item.title: Albanese and Taylor both looking to history for future of Australia's tax system
item.description: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

item.title: Developers with state-tied projects donate to Queensland politicians after ban lifted
item.description: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

item.title: Taiwanese president says island will not be sacrificed after Trump-Xi meeting
item.description: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

item.title: State of Origin: Why did Billy Slater leave Reece Walsh out of his Game I Queensland Maroons team?
item.description: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

item.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'
item.description: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.

item.title: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown
item.description: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.

item.title: The Talented Mr F follows two filmmakers trying to find their stolen movie
item.description: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

item.title: How to deal with money stress in your relationship
item.description: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

item.title: Should the tax system reward those who can afford to take risks?
item.description: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

item.title: Shallow burials, landfill: Where tonnes of Tasmania's dead salmon went
item.description: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

item.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child in Australia
item.description: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

item.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle over OpenAI
item.description: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.

item.title: TISM fined for $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House ahead of tour
item.description: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

item.title: Undersea volcano erupts in Papua New Guinea's Bismarck Sea prompting tsunami concerns
item.description: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Angine de Poitrine, the French Canadian costumed microtonal duo breaking the internet
item.description: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

item.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
item.description: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.

item.title: Life on the islands of the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran war
item.description: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

item.title: Vanuatu agrees to pacts with China and Australia
item.description: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.

item.title: Small businesses and startups go viral with AI Albo but tax experts 'rubbish' CGT claims
item.description: One startup founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.

item.title: Contentious developments receiving fast-tracked approval under affordable housing pathway
item.description: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

item.title: Can alternative content help to fight the influence of the 'manosphere'?
item.description: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

item.title: After booting Hawthorn out of Tasmania, the Devils must deliver for Launceston
item.description: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

item.title: Arsenal wins Premier League after 22-year drought following Manchester City's draw against Bournemouth
item.description: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

item.title: Israel says no live ammunition used by troops filmed firing at Gaza flotilla boats
item.description: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

item.title: Plans to revive WA's once-booming nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices
item.description: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

item.title: AVG Travels fallout deepens as more customers report cancelled or rescheduled tours
item.description: The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 travellers since first reporting on the matter on Monday, with some claiming their tours were cancelled at the last minute and itineraries changed.

item.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one
item.description: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

item.title: Ocean Census discovers over 1,100 new marine species
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jai Arrow, South Sydney and Queensland Maroons star, retires from NRL due to motor neurone disease
item.description: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

item.title: Thailand cuts visa-free stay period for more than 90 countries, citing crimes by foreigners
item.description: Thailand is reducing the visa-free period available to visitors from 90 countries, including Australia, with the duration decided on a country-by-country basis.

item.title: The Inland Rail is paused but the father of it has a plan to see it through to completion by his 100th birthday
item.description: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

item.title: Vladimir Putin meets 'old friend' Xi Jinping in Beijing and secures more than 20 agreements
item.description: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.

item.title: Finnish divers recover remaining two bodies of Italians from underwater cave in Maldives
item.description: Finnish divers have recovered the remaining bodies of the divers that died in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

item.title: Far-right Israeli minister posts video of him taunting detained activists from Global Sumud Flotilla
item.description: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

item.title: Why renovating your outdoor area gives 'the best bang for your buck'
item.description: Adding a new kitchen or bathroom to your home can be expensive, but budget constraints don't mean you have to take home improvement off the table altogether.

item.title: Music quiz: Can you pick the classical music in these iconic movies?
item.description: From Mozart to Max Richter, can you pick the classical music in these iconic films?

item.title: Can The Mandalorian and Grogu save Star Wars?
item.description: Grogu (aka Baby Yoda) made The Mandalorian a hit on streaming, now Disney is looking to parlay that goodwill into ticket sales to the latest Star Wars cinematic release.

item.title: Pressure builds on new-home industry as Iran war sends construction costs through the roof
item.description: Amid fast-rising prices — from materials craned in, to rubbish trucked out — a residential builder lists the costlier surcharges, levies and products hammering the construction industry and fuelling inflation.

item.title: The majority of Tuvalu has applied to relocate to Australia to escape climate change. What happens now?
item.description: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.

item.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme
item.description: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

item.title: BOM winter forecast predicts unusually warm season as El Niño increasingly likely
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

item.title: Political cost of budget tax plan emerges as Labor focuses on future
item.description: In an already difficult post-budget week, Labor isn't being helped by some of its "friends". But it has won an important endorsement from one powerful voice.

item.title: 'Short-term pain' to wrestle Tasmanian budget back to black in two years, Abetz says
item.description: There will be "short-term pain" to get Tasmania's finances back in the black within two years, state treasurer Eric Abetz says ahead of today's budget.

item.title: Donald Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia, David Brat, questioned by US senators
item.description: David Brat, who rose to political prominence as part of the conservative Tea Party movement, takes questions from senators at a confirmation hearing.

item.title: Fear in Karumba fishing industry after shock closure as locals wonder what's on horizon
item.description: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".

item.title: Calls to support struggling parents as national survey reveals increasing psychological distress
item.description: As the birth rate hits a record low it's been revealed that almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national parenting survey.

item.title: Critics argue government's response to aged care price caps 'a total joke'
item.description: Some advocates say the government's decision to delay price caps for at-home aged care services is a "burden".

item.title: A year on from record floods, NSW Mid North Coast residents are returning home
item.description: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mid North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

item.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's face has been revealed. Here's why the suppression lifted
item.description: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

item.title: Photographer accused of stealing Bondi terror attack victim Peter Meagher's camera
item.description: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

item.title: Strathmerton fears it will become 'ghost town' as Bega Cheese factory closure nears
item.description: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

item.title: Iran examining US proposal as Donald Trump says talks in 'final stages'
item.description: Iran says it is examining the latest US proposal received from mediator Pakistan in talks to end the Middle East war, despite the two sides trading threats over resuming attacks.

item.title: Live: Singtel wants to sell minority stake in Optus, unemployment rate spikes
item.description: Optus owner Singtel says it is looking to sell a minority stake in it. And the jobless rate has jumped to 4.5 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing will pay more tax under CGT changes
item.description: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

item.title: Congo football team cancels Kinshasa World Cup farewell as Ebola outbreak concerns grow
item.description: The Congo football team cancels its farewell in Kinshasa as the US clarified what the rules would be for players and staff entering the country for the World Cup.

item.title: How the 'King of the North' Andy Burnham could topple UK PM Sir Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham is angling to become the UK's next prime minister. He has plenty of popularity, but no seat in parliament. That could be about to change.

item.title: Abrolhos Islands coral reef shows resistance to climate bleaching, study finds
item.description: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

item.title: Retiring NRL star Jai Arrow says he will fight after shock MND diagnosis
item.description: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

item.title: Two Russian jets intercepts British Royal Air Force surveillance plane
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: UK Rivet Joint aircraft repeatedly and dangerously intercepted by Russian jets over Black Sea
item.description: Two Russian jets have intercepted a Royal Air Force plane over the Black Sea, flying close enough to trigger its emergency systems.

item.title: Donald Trump's latest trophy proves his power over his party — despite the polls
item.description: Donald Trump's revenge tour is in full swing, and his latest victim is a Republican who has voted with his party more than 90 per cent of the time.

item.title: What does broken heart syndrome feel like?
item.description: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

item.title: Prince William goes wild as Aston Villa claims Europa League title and ends 30-year drought
item.description: In front of a joyful future king of England, Aston Villa coach Unai Emery reaffirmed his status as the king of the Europa League as Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Istanbul to secure Emery a record-extending fifth title in the Europa League.

item.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns
item.description: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

item.title: Elon Musk's X Corp ordered to pay $750,000 after admitting it contravened Australian child protection request
item.description: Elon ​Musk's social media company X Corp has ​admitted ‌to not complying with an order from Australia's eSafety commissioner to ⁠provide information about the measures it has in place ​to stop ‌child exploitation on the X platform.

item.title: On the eve of Stephen Colbert's final Late Show, America is asking: Can late night survive the Trump administration?
item.description: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

item.title: Joey Chestnut to defend hot-dog-eating crown on probation after battery plea
item.description: The competitive-eating sensation was accused of slapping a man across the face during a late night out at an Indiana bar, according to Us Weekly.

item.title: Tech giant Nvidia reports growth of 85 per cent due to the construction of AI factories
item.description: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

item.title: Former NSW Young Australian of the Year Nikhil Autar dies aged 32
item.description: Nikhil Autar, who was diagnosed with leukaemia when he was 17 years old, was named NSW Young Australian of the Year in 2024.

item.title: Queensland Olympics Minister Tim Mander referred to AFP over electoral enrolment status
item.description: The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) confirmed on Thursday it had made the referral.

item.title: Unemployment rate jumps to 4.5 per cent, driven by female unemployment
item.description: The unemployment rate rose by 0.2 percentage points to 4.5 per cent in April, up from 4.3 per cent in March, according to the Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the most important satirists in American history
item.description: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

item.title: Football Australia posts record losses as plan to axe 20 per cent of staff revealed
item.description: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

item.title: The deadly 1996 plane downing at the heart of Raúl Castro's indictment
item.description: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.

item.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
item.description: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

item.title: Kylie doco is a human portrait of pop royalty's resilience and reinvention
item.description: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

item.title: Woman accused of hit-and-run murder of Kiesha Thompson at Daisy Hill committed to stand trial
item.description: Larissa Rita Mae-Leigh Sant is accused of planning to kill her then-partner's ex-girlfriend, Kiesha Thompson, before striking her with a car while she was walking.

item.title: Rockhampton Plaza Hotel sells for $5.75 million at court-ordered auction
item.description: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

item.title: Man convicted of Irma Palasics's death was previously jailed over million-dollar cigarette conspiracy
item.description: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

item.title: Peak body slams ministers' absence from Aboriginal housing and homelands conference as 'disgraceful'
item.description: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

item.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction in Adelaide as SA party opposes fracking proposal
item.description: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

item.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

item.title: Family's story of dementia shines spotlight on Australia's leading cause of death
item.description: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

item.title: Jai Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure
item.description: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

item.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from paedophile Peter Liddy
item.description: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

item.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine green-lit as federal government agrees to buy 500 tonnes of product per year
item.description: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

item.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Eric Abetz looks to rein in spending
item.description: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

item.title: Fears for Australia's climate change adaptability as CSIRO cuts over 90 environment research jobs
item.description: Some CSIRO staff are concerned the science agency's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

item.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Where are the cuts coming from?
item.description: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

item.title: Final hearing in Queensland inquiry held as submissions claim child safety complaints were ignored
item.description: Final hearings have concluded at Queensland's inquiry into child safety, with a "lengthy" report expected to be delivered to the government on Friday.

item.title: Perth and WA council mergers are on the table again — here's what happened last time
item.description: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: The Besties say violent podcast clip is 'just one example of what we deal with' as female creators
item.description: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

item.title: Coles investigates Apple gift card scam after Melbourne artist's viral complaint
item.description: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about buying faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

item.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers
item.description: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

item.title: Defence minister says government will fight Landbridge lawsuit over Darwin port ownership
item.description: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

item.title: Public transport to stay free for an additional year in Tasmania
item.description: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.

item.title: What you need to know about tomorrow's strike by ACT public school teachers and staff
item.description: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff are going on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that will see all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.

item.title: NDIS integrity inquiry hears raft of alarming claims and calls for greater whistleblower protections
item.description: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.

item.title: Isabelle Kelly returns to chase an Origin dynasty 10 years after breakthrough Blues win
item.description: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.

item.title: Man sentenced for killing Ian Welldon in Toowoomba truck and motorcycle crash
item.description: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.
